params.name: Zeno’s Paradoxes
params.description: Zeno of Elea posed four paradoxes, most famously Achilles and the tortoise, and the flying arrow. Using logical deduction, he challenged intuitive views of motion and continuity. His ideas shaped Aristotle’s physics and foreshadowed calculus in the Enlightenment. Even today, echoes of his reasoning appear in quantum theory debates.
